name: Kingdom of Mysore (1799–1947)

colors.symbolism: the darker band and the Gandaberunda

description: A red-and-maroon field charged with the Gandaberunda — a stylised two-headed bird emblem — in the centre.
history: Flag of the Kingdom of Mysore under the Wadiyar dynasty (after the death of Tipu Sultan in 1799). The Gandaberunda is an ancient mythological two-headed bird from Hindu/Vaishnav tradition, associated with the Wadiyars since the 16th century. The kingdom acceded to the Indian Union in 1947 and was incorporated into the new Mysore State.

features.symbolism: the heraldic emblem of the Wadiyar dynasty of Mysore — symbolises strength and the dual aspect of divine power
